items.title: Microsoft Defender Zero-Days CVE-2026-41091 & CVE-2026-45498 Chained in Live Attacks
items.summary: Two actively exploited Defender flaws are being chained: CVE-2026-45498 (CVSS 4.0) disables Defender via DoS, then CVE-2026-41091 (CVSS 7.8) escalates the crippled host to SYSTEM via improper link resolution in the Malware Protection Engine. CISA set a federal patching deadline of June 3, 2026; fixed in Defender Antimalware Platform 4.18.26040.7.
items.why: Blinding your EDR before privilege escalation is a lethal combination. The attack chain is already in the wild and every unpatched Windows endpoint running Defender is at risk — federal agencies have under 72 hours to patch.
items.source: The Hacker News / BleepingComputer / CISA KEV

items.title: CVE-2026-31431 'Copy Fail' — Linux Kernel LPE Earns CISA KEV with Working Public Exploit
items.summary: A 9-year-old logic flaw in the Linux kernel's AF_ALG cryptographic interface (algif_aead module) lets any unprivileged local user gain root via a controlled 4-byte kernel page-cache overwrite. CVSS 7.8. Affects Ubuntu, RHEL, Amazon Linux, and virtually every mainstream distro since 2017. A reliable working exploit was public at the time of CISA KEV addition. Patches: kernels 6.18.22, 6.19.12, 7.0.
items.why: A reliable, publicly available root exploit against nearly every enterprise Linux distro is as severe as local-privilege bugs get. Cloud workloads and containers sharing a host kernel are particularly exposed.
items.source: CISA KEV / The Hacker News / SafeBreach / Microsoft Security Blog

items.title: CVE-2026-34926: Trend Micro Apex One Directory Traversal Turns AV Update Channel Into Malware Distributor
items.summary: A directory traversal flaw (CWE-23) in Trend Micro Apex One on-premise servers allows an authenticated admin to modify a core server table and inject malicious code, which is then silently pushed to all managed endpoints through the trusted Apex One update mechanism. CISA added it to KEV on May 21, 2026 after Trend Micro confirmed at least one in-the-wild exploitation attempt. Fix: SP1 Critical Patch Build 18012.
items.why: Security products are the highest-value supply-chain target in any enterprise. Compromising the Apex One server converts the AV update pipeline into a stealthy malware delivery network across every protected endpoint in the organisation.
items.source: CISA KEV / The Hacker News / Help Net Security / Trend Micro

items.title: Iranian APT Screening Serpens Deploys MiniUpdate & MiniJunk V2 RATs via Fake Airline Job Portals
items.summary: Palo Alto Unit42 tracked Iran-linked Screening Serpens (active since 2022) targeting the US, Israel, and UAE between February and April 2026 with two new RAT families: MiniUpdate and MiniJunk V2. Infection chains begin with spear phishing via fake airline hiring portals delivering malware-laden ZIPs, using DLL sideloading and AppDomainManager hijacking. C2 traffic is routed through three-to-five Azure-hosted domains unique per target.
items.why: The use of legitimate Azure cloud infrastructure for C2 makes network-level blocking extremely difficult. Aerospace, defence manufacturing, telecom, and tech sectors are squarely in the crosshairs alongside US and Israeli government targets amid ongoing regional conflict.

items.title: APT28 Operation Neusploit: Fancy Bear Weaponizes CVE-2026-21509 in 3 Days, Targets European Military & Government
items.summary: Russia's GRU-linked APT28 (Fancy Bear) weaponized Microsoft Office security-bypass CVE-2026-21509 only three days after public disclosure, launching spear-phishing attacks against defence ministries, maritime operators, and diplomatic entities across Poland, Slovakia, Romania, Ukraine, Turkey, Greece, and the UAE. The multi-stage chain delivers the BEARDSHELL backdoor via cloud-hosted LNK files, DLL sideloading, and in-memory execution. CERT-UA officially attributed the campaign to UAC-0001.
items.why: A 3-day exploit-to-weaponisation window by a nation-state actor underscores the vanishing patch window for high-profile Office flaws. Nation-state targeting of NATO-aligned defence ministries signals continued escalation of Russian cyber operations in the European theatre.
